message:
perf(raptorq,scheduler): revert mul/addmul 16→8-byte batches and add scheduler fast-paths

Benchmark-driven corrections and new optimizations:

gf256.rs - Revert mul_with_table_wide and addmul_with_table_wide from
16-byte back to 8-byte batches. The 16-byte widening caused 1.4-1.7x
regressions on mul_slice (all sizes) and addmul_slice (16384) due to
excessive load-port pressure from 16 dependent table lookups per
iteration. The 32-byte add_slice widening (4×u64 XOR) is retained as
it provides a consistent 1.2-1.3x speedup for sizes ≥ 1024.

priority.rs - Add #[inline] to Ord/PartialOrd impls for SchedulerEntry
and TimedEntry (called on every BinaryHeap sift). Add single-element
fast-path in pop() using peek()+clear() to avoid O(log n) sift when
lane has exactly one entry. Add early-exit in pop_entry_with_rng and
pop_timed_with_rng when lane is empty or next entry differs in
priority/deadline, avoiding unnecessary scratch Vec allocation.
Batch benchmarks: 1.4-1.8x faster at 100-1000 elements.

global_injector.rs - Add #[inline] to TimedTask Ord/PartialOrd.
arena.rs - Add #[inline] to ArenaIndex Hash impl.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
